ops.title: Never edit files in a worktree an active delegated lane is using — lanes revert \"unexpected\" changes as their own side effects
ops.body:
While a codex fix lane worked in /tmp/capthook-wsA, the orchestrator concurrently wrote
docs into the same worktree (disjoint files: CHANGELOG, qmd guides). The lane's cleanup
step saw those changes, classified them as its own accidental side effects (its
instructions said "do NOT touch CHANGELOG.md or docs/"), and **reverted them all**. The
orchestrator had to detect the loss and re-apply every edit.

**Why:** a delegated lane treats its worktree as exclusively owned. Any diff it didn't
make looks like an accident to clean up — file-level disjointness does not protect you,
because "don't touch X" instructions convert your concurrent edits to X into the lane's
cleanup targets.

**How to apply:** one writer per worktree at a time. Queue prose/docs edits for a shared
worktree until every active lane in it reports done (or write them elsewhere and copy
in). Related: [[parallel-sessions-own-the-working-copy]]. Also from the same session:
`gh run list --commit <sha>` lags run registration by minutes — a watch loop that exits
green on an empty run list is a vacuous watch; guard it (fail loudly when zero runs
register) and prefer resolving run ids first, then `gh run watch <id> --exit-status`.
